results.caption: DragonForce isn't a hacker. It's a business. Launched on BreachForums December 6, 2023 — within 18 months it had carved up 363 victims across 30+ countries, absorbed RansomHub, and was running an 80/20 affiliate revenue split like a Fortune 500 franchise. This is what industrialized ransomware looks like when it grows up. Study the model. Know the enemy. 🐉 #Ransomware #DragonForce #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythologized threat actor portrait that makes cybersecurity professionals stop scrolling — the dragon as corporate cartel boss demands attention.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption: On a Tuesday morning in May 2025, Marks & Spencer employees couldn't process a payment. By the time IT understood what had happened, DragonForce had deployed ChaCha8 across Windows, Linux, and ESXi — encrypting M&S, Co-op, and Harrods simultaneously. Estimated impact: £300M+. The ransom note wasn't a threat. It was an invoice. 🔐 #DragonForce #Ransomware #MarksAndSpencer #IncidentResponse #CyberAttack
results.hook: A ransomware note rendered as crime-scene forensic evidence triggers visceral dread — retail executives who lived through M&S will recognize the feeling immediately.

results.caption: Where does DragonForce come from? Malaysia? A rebranded LockBit? A new collective? Nobody can confirm. What we know: it debuted on BreachForums on December 6, 2023. By March 19, 2025, it announced itself as a full cartel. By April 8, 2025, it had absorbed RansomHub. Attribution remains open. The fog is the feature — not the bug. 🌐 #ThreatIntelligence #DragonForce #CyberWarfare #Attribution #Ransomware
results.hook: An unresolved attribution mystery wrapped in geopolitical fog — the 'who built this weapon?' question drives massive engagement from threat intel professionals and executives alike.
